commit.message:
fix: add @Prop to selectedValue/hintFocusIndex for reactive parent→child sync (#2)

* Initial plan

* fix: add @Prop to selectedValue and hintFocusIndex for reactive updates

In ArkUI, properties without @Prop are initialization-only and don't
update when the parent re-renders. This caused selectedValue to be stuck
at 0, so same-number cells could never get the distinctive green color
and instead rendered with the peer-highlight color (light blue), making
them appear "selected".

commit.message:
fix: filter postamble passages (closing signatures, adjournments, votes)

Extended _is_preamble_passage() with three new signals:
  - Ends with 'Secretary' on its own line (closing signature block)
  - Contains 'The meeting adjourned' (adjournment notice)
  - Contains 'Votes against:' (voting record)

These postamble fragments slipped through because they appear at the
end of old-format minutes (full-text fallback), not the opening.
All 4 junk passages removed; 2 legitimate short sentences kept.

Final counts: 286 GM / 238 PCZ / 210 PC (734 total), 12/12 pairs.

commit.message:
Add allocation type export and fix strict-mode crashes in analyzers

Export sampled allocation tick data from PerfView ETL using
SaveManagedHeapAllocStacksAsCsv. The data was being collected
(/DotNetAllocSampled) but never extracted  now exported as
alloc-types-folded.txt and passed via AllocTypesPath.

Fix strict-mode terminating errors in memory-gc and cpu-hotspots
analyzers: replace direct property access on hashtables (which
throws under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest) with ContainsKey
checks. Allocation data is now gracefully optional with a log
message when absent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Emit XML doc comments from schema descriptions in C# codegen (#724)

Update the C# code generator (csharp.ts) to emit /// <summary> XML doc
comments on all generated types and members, sourced from JSON Schema
description annotations.

Changes to scripts/codegen/csharp.ts:
- Add escapeXml(), ensureTrailingPunctuation(), xmlDocComment(),
  rawXmlDocSummary(), and xmlDocCommentWithFallback() helpers
- Emit <summary> on all data classes, nested classes, polymorphic base
  and derived classes, enum types, and enum members
- Emit <remarks> with event name on SessionEvent-derived classes when a
  real schema description is present
- Emit <inheritdoc /> on all override string Type properties
- Emit <see cref> cross-references in data class fallback summaries
- Use <c> tags for event names and discriminator values
- Ensure all comments end with sentence-ending punctuation
- Add synthetic fallback summaries for types without schema descriptions
- Apply XML escaping to schema-sourced text; skip escaping for
  codegen-controlled XML tags

Regenerated output (from published @github/copilot v1.0.2 schemas):
- SessionEvents.cs: ~525 <summary> + 69 <inheritdoc> comments
- Rpc.cs: class-level and property-level docs with fallbacks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Added balance_passages: false to config.yaml passage block.

Default is false: let the LLM naturally filter procedural GM passages

counts post-extraction.

commit.message:
Harden PerfView collectors for long runs

- Add /NoNGenPdbs to both collectors  skips NGEN PDB generation during
  merge (saves 4-10s per pass, not needed for folded stacks or GC stats)
- Add .etl.log.txt and .etl.zip.abort to stale file cleanup in both
  Start-Collector scripts  prevents false-positive completion detection
  from leftover files in interrupted prior runs
- Verify kill success in both Stop-Collector scripts  warn if PerfView
  process fails to terminate instead of silently continuing, preventing
  undetected orphaned process accumulation over long runs (100+ experiments)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: cmake wrapper to inject CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM for boolector deps

setup-btor2tools.sh invokes cmake directly on btor2tools, which has an
old cmake_minimum_required incompatible with modern CMake. Use a cmake
wrapper script that prepends -DCMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5 to all
cmake invocations so both btor2tools and boolector configure correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: commit before pull --rebase to prevent staged-changes failure

The previous order (git add → pull --rebase → commit) always fails when
there are staged changes, since git refuses to rebase with uncommitted
work. New order: git add → commit → pull --rebase → push.

This was the root cause of all sync failures since Mar 6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Evaluation] Fix UTF-8 encoding for red team JSONL files on Windows (#45500)

* Fix UTF-8 encoding for red team JSONL files on Windows

Add explicit encoding='utf-8' to all file open() calls in the PyRIT result
processing path. Without this, Windows defaults to the system locale encoding
(charmap/cp1252), causing UnicodeDecodeError when reading JSONL files containing
non-ASCII characters from UnicodeConfusable strategy or CJK languages.

Fixes: Tests 1.7 (UnicodeConfusable), 1.16 (Japanese/Chinese)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add encoding regression tests for non-ASCII JSONL round-trip

Test CJK characters, Unicode confusables, and mixed scripts to prevent
future regressions of the charmap encoding bug on Windows.

commit.message:
Fix PerfView stop: poll log for completion instead of blind WaitForExit

PerfView /NoGui has a known bug where the process lingers after completing
all work (collection, rundown, merge, zip). Previously, Stop-Collector
blocked on WaitForExit for the full StopTimeoutSec (300s default), wasting
~5 minutes per collector pass even though data was ready in ~40s.

Now polls PerfView's log file every 3s for the '[DONE' completion marker.
Once detected:
- 5s grace period for natural exit
- Clean termination if still lingering (data confirmed safe)
- ETW session cleanup only needed in the true timeout fallback path

This reduces the stop phase from ~300s to ~45s per collector pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 48: Zero-alloc appendFgColorPacked/appendBgColorPacked — eliminate Color DU heap allocation

Previously appendFgColorPacked/appendBgColorPacked called PackedColor.unpack
which allocated a Color discriminated union case on the heap (heap-allocated
object per cell boundary in the render hot path). This partially undermined
the zero-alloc claim of Presenter.presentInto.

Fix: inline the packed int32 bit extraction directly in both functions.
Each branch matches on the 2-bit tag and reads BaseColor/Intensity/Rgb/Ansi256
fields directly from the int32 without constructing any .NET heap objects.
This eliminates one heap allocation per style-boundary cell in the hot path.

PackedColor bit layout (unchanged):
  bits 0-1: tag (0=Default, 1=Named, 2=Ansi256, 3=Rgb)
  Named bits 2-4: BaseColor index, bit 5: Intensity (0=Normal, 1=Bright)
  Ansi256 bits 8-15: palette index
  Rgb bits 8-15=R, 16-23=G, 24-31=B

Tests: 12 new property + unit tests in sprint48PackedColorTests verifying
appendFgColorPacked/appendBgColorPacked produce identical ANSI output to
the DU-based appendFgColor/appendBgColor for all 4 color tag cases.

Total: 2,055 tests passing.

commit.message:
Refactor database access to use context manager connections

Replace persistent global database connection with on-demand connections
via context manager. This prevents long-lived locks on duckdb file,
allowing SSH access and manual database inspection on Render.

Changes:
- db/connection.py: get_connection() now a context manager that opens/closes per query
- db/schema.py: Use context manager for schema creation
- db/cached_queries.py: Wrap all con.sql() calls with context manager
- All page files (03, 04, 13, 14, 15, 20): Use context manager for SQL operations

Benefits:
- Database file not locked between queries
- Allows SSH access to inspect/query database on Render
- Proper resource cleanup with context managers

commit.message:
Add --apk flag to extract base APK from XAPK bundles

When downloading split APK bundles (XAPK), the --apk flag automatically
extracts the base APK and removes the XAPK wrapper. This makes downloads
directly usable with tools like ReVanced that need a plain APK.

Also adds extract_base_apk() to the client module for programmatic use.

commit.message:
feat/chezmoi migration (#3)

* feat: add chezmoi foundation with gitconfig/zshrc/mise templates

Phase 1 of dotfiles migration to chezmoi + mise architecture.

- Add .chezmoiroot pointing to home/ as source directory
- Add .chezmoi.toml.tmpl with platform detection (OS, WSL, Codespaces)
- Add .chezmoiignore to skip platform-irrelevant files per OS
- Convert 6 gitconfig files to chezmoi templates:
  - dot_gitconfig.tmpl: base config, REMOVED [safe] directory = *
  - dot_gitconfig-linux.tmpl: merged WSL + native (was 2 files)
  - dot_gitconfig-{mac,windows,corp}.tmpl: parameterized paths/usernames
- Convert zshrc to template (fixed hardcoded terraform path)
- Add .mise.toml under chezmoi management
- Add install.sh for Codespaces/first-time bootstrap

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: unify Copilot Guard hooks into single Python script

Phase 2-1: Replace dual bash/PowerShell implementation with cross-platform Python.

- Single copilot-guard.py replaces copilot-guard.sh (151 lines) + .ps1 (173 lines)
- Absorbs Windows stdin encoding difference (UTF-8 reconfigure + BOM strip)
- Eliminates jq dependency (uses Python built-in json module)
- PEP 723 inline metadata for uv compatibility (requires-python >= 3.9)
- hooks.json updated to use 'uv run' for both bash and powershell entries
- All check logic preserved: path checks, boundary-aware command checks, URL allowlist
- Fail-safe deny on any error (matches original design)
- chezmoi deploys to ~/.copilot/hooks/ automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add copilot-instructions.md (user-level and repo-level)

Phase 2-2: Custom instructions for GitHub Copilot.

- User-level (~/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md): language prefs, coding style,
  shell/Python conventions, infrastructure preferences
- Repo-level (.github/copilot-instructions.md): chezmoi architecture, template
  variables, hook system, tool management conventions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: convert bootstrap scripts to chezmoi run_once_ templates

Phase 3: Migrate setup/*.sh and bootstrap.sh into chezmoi lifecycle.

run_once_before_:
- 10-install-packages: apt (Linux) / brew (macOS) system packages
- 20-install-mise: mise installation with brew fallback on macOS

run_once_after_:
- 10-setup-shell: Oh My Zsh + zsh-completions plugin + default shell
- 20-mise-install: mise install (tool versions from .mise.toml)
- 30-install-tools: Azure CLI, azd, Docker, Rust, Go tools, Copilot CLI,
  draw.io — with full OS/WSL/Codespaces conditional logic

Key improvements:
- Azure CLI ARM64: removed obsolete pip workaround, uses native APT packages
- Docker: skipped in WSL (Docker Desktop handles it)
- Codespaces/DevContainer: 30-install-tools entirely skipped
- Removed brew install python@3 (Python managed by uv)
- Unified Linux/macOS into single templates with OS branching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 4: Windows integration.

- Move configuration.dsc.yaml and winterm-settings.json to reference/windows/
